title: (Non-UDG treated) double-stranded modern dental calculus DNA library preparation for Illumina sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ie4d","text":"Protocol for the preparation of double-stranded genomic libraries for Illumina sequencing. This produces adapter ligated DNA fragments that can be used in conjunction with downstream Indexing protocols.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"38e0t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"245dh","text":"Working in an Ancient DNA Laboratory","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dt05o","text":"- All steps of the protocol (except the qPCR at the end) should take place in a clean room facility specifically designed for ancient DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7jbik","text":"- The researcher performing lab work should be dressed in correspondingly suitable lab-wear, such as:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e3do3","text":"   - full-body suit with hood (e.g., Tyvek)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6o3e9","text":"   - hairnet","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"54gcv","text":"   - face mask","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qa4s","text":"   - two pairs of clean gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7557","text":"   - clean shoes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fh00o","text":"   - protective glasses","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aoi5d","text":"- Sample processing should be carried out in separated work benches with integrated UV irradiation (e.g. Dead Air PCR work bench)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cefsa","text":"- Surfaces and equipment should be regularly decontaminated with e.g. bleach solution or Thermofisher's DNA AWAY (or similar) and irradiated with UV.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9e3df","text":"- All home-made buffers should be prepared in a seperate decidated PCR-free ultra-clean room and UV-irradiated for 30 min.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bhoth","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fkp0o","text":"Please see the following for more detailed guidance: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9fhjr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"afnjk","text":"Llamas, B. et al., 2017. From the field to the laboratory: Controlling DNA contamination in human ancient DNA research in the high-throughput sequencing era. STAR: Science \u0026 Technology of Archaeological Research, 3(1), pp.1–14. Available at: https://doi.org/10.1080/20548923.2016.1258824.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":158,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":242,"length":45}],"data":[]},{"key":"168mn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"31ihp","text":"Working in an Molecular Biology Laboratory","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"89kc8","text":"The qPCR reaction takes place in a standard DNA-based molecular biology lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"af43l","text":"Please keep in mind the safety guidelines of your specific country and institution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dse84","text":"Recommendations include wearing of:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dhn1u","text":"- lab coats","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ffhep","text":"- closed shoes and trousers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqlc8","text":"- safety glasses","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"62b2d","text":"- nitrile or latex gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7sipt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ak3pq","text":"Protocol Specific Guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cnsh9","text":"This protocol requires the use of two rooms - a dedicated PCR-free ultra-clean library building room and a standard molecular biology lab for qPCR.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"AE2E68F7910511EABAE40A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://doi.org/10.1080/20548923.2016.1258824"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f0lgc","text":"Planning","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dchsa","text":"This protocol takes approximately 6 hours.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d4jkl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"93e4j","text":"While all steps of the protocol are performed in an Ancient DNA facility, the qPCR will be performed in a modern DNA facility with a 1:10 dilution of the samples, which were prepared in the cleanroom facilities. The library remains in the cleanroom for further processing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dteg6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8psda","text":"Check waste disposal guidance for all reagents in this protocol against your corresponding laboratory regulations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a55b7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"15bvd","text":"Preparation of reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cmm82","text":"All home-made buffers should be prepared in a separate dedicated PCR-free ultra-clean room and UV-irradiated for 30 min. Purchased kits should be DNA-free.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5dnpb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d2cf2","text":"Qiagen MinElute kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"560l4","text":"Add ethanol to PE wash buffer acccording to manufacturer's instructions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"23hok","text":"Add Tween-20 to EB elution buffer to a final concentration of 0.05% (Tween-20 in EB). This solution is referred to as EBT throughout the protocol. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":118,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5t3gg","text":"All reagents of MinElute PCR Purification Kit should be decontaminated with a 30 min UV irradiation before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":78,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2nevr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"56i2v","text":" HPLC-Water should be decontaminated with a 30 min UV irradiation before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":44,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40u2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"114ri","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fdn5l","text":"Make sure all necessary equipment is available (see Materials).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"n7og","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tffq","text":"Abbreviations","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9baie","text":"EBT = EB elution buffer with 0.05% Tween-20","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6k5dj","text":"GuHCl = Guanidinium chloride or Guanidine hydrochloride","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"an8ri","text":"HPLC = High Performance Liquid Chromatography (-Grade Water)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9njc3","text":"NaClO = Sodium hypochlorite","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fcl17","text":"UV = Ultraviolet (radiation)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cra6l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b368b","text":"Controls","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ou71","text":"Take along extraction positive and negative (blank) controls generated during your extraction (see example Extraction Protocol dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baksicwe)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":127,"length":41}],"data":[]},{"key":"fih4p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fol2j","text":"For this protocol you should also add another negative control (HPLC water) to monitor the library preparation experiment. Consider these three extra samples in your calculations for mastermixes and buffer preparations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"947d3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f4kv4","text":"Additional Tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8fr8h","text":"It is recommended to prepare 10% more of the calculated volume of all mastermixes to compensate for possible pipetting error.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"CACB6A6DCDA411EAAB070A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baksicwe"}}}}

steps: Prepare a mastermix for the blunt end repair calculating 50 µL, / reaction . Use a new 1.5 ml LoBind tube to set up the mastermix. A B C D 1 Reagent Stock concentration Final concentration 1x Volume [µl] 2 NEB Buffer 2 10 x 1 x 5 3 ATP 10 mM 1 mM 5 4 BSA 20 mg/ml 0.8 mg/ml 2 5 dNTPs 25 mM each 0.25 mM 0.5 6 T4 PNK 10 U 0.4 U 2 7 T4 Polymerase 3 U 0.024 U 0.4 8 UV HPLC-water 15.1 9 DNA or UV HPLC-water 20 10 Total 50 Do not add the DNA to the mastermix if you set it up for 2 or more reactions! You can up to 20 µL Extract, but with a maximum Input of 100 ng DNA in total.
Add 30 µL mastermix to each reaction tube and then add 20 µL sample DNA to each tube (use 0.2 ml PCR tube strips). Mix by flicking the tubes and spin down briefly.

Incubate at 25 °C for 00:20:00 , then at 12 °C for 00:10:00 in the thermocycler. During this incubation take MinElute tubes out of the fridge so that they warm to room temperature before use in the next step. Also label two sets of 1.5 ml LoBind tubes per sample and one set of 0.2 ml PCR tubes for the next steps. At this time you can also add the PB-Buffer (650µl) to one set of 1.5 ml LoBind tubes for Step 3.2.
Purify the blunt-end repaired DNA with a MinElute kit, with the following modifications to the manufacturer's protocol.
Pre-heat elution buffer EBT to 50 °C .
For each reaction, add 650 µL PB (binding) buffer to a new 1.5 ml LoBind tube and add the blunt end repaired sample, then vortex briefly to mix.
Load each reaction (PB buffer + blunt-end repaired sample) onto a MinElute column and incubate at RT for 00:02:00 . This allows sufficient time for the DNA to bind to the silica membrane.

Spin 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flow-through. Discard flow-through in one of two following ways: Remove all liquid in the collection tube with a pipette, or Pour off the liquid into a fresh waste tube, and pat the rim of the collection tube dry on a paper tissue or towel. Use just one spot on the paper tissue per sample. Be careful not to touch the rim of the tube on the waste container. After you are finished with all samples, discard the paper and wipe clean the surface underneath with water and then sterilize the surface with bleach (or DNA Away). During cleaning of surfaces after flow-through discard, do NOT apply bleach before first cleaning with water. Residual GuHCl will react with bleach to form toxic gases.
Add 700 µL PE (wash) buffer to the MinElute column.
Spin at 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flow-through as in step 3.4 .
Dry spin for 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Flip columns 180° and dry spin again for 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Remove columns from their collection tubes and place them in new 1.5 ml LoBind tubes.

Add 20 µL pre-heated EBT to the column, let stand for 00:01:00 then spin 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute to elute. Carefully pipette EBT directly onto the center of the membrane without touching the membrane.

Prepare a mastermix for adapter ligation calculating 40 µL, / reaction . Use a 1.5 ml LoBind tube to set up the ligation mastermix. The mastermix is calculated to accommodate addition of Quick Ligase to each reaction individually after aliquotting to individual reaction tubes. %s %s
Aliquot 21 µL of mastermix to each tube (use 0.2 ml PCR strips). Transfer the complete eluate (step 3.10) (~ 18 µL ) per sample to each new tube.

Then add 1 µL of Quick ligase ( 5 U stock, 0.125 U final concentration) to each library sample. Mix by flicking the tubes and spin down. Make sure to add the full volume of ligase by pipetting up and down several times to get all of it off the walls of the pipette tip.

Incubate at 22 °C for 00:20:00 in the thermocycler. During this incubation take MinElute tubes out of the fridge so that they warm to room temperature before use in the next step. Also label two sets of 1.5 ml LoBind tubes and 0.2 ml PCR tubes per sample for the next steps. At this time you can also add the PB-Buffer (650µl) to one set of 1.5 ml LoBind tubes for Step 7.2.
Purify the adapter-ligated libraries with a MinElute kit, with the following modifications to the manufacturer's instructions
Pre-heat EBT to 50 °C .
For each sample, add 650 µL PB buffer to a new 1.5 ml LoBind tube and add the entire volume of the adapter ligation mix, then vortex briefly.
Load each reaction (PB buffer + adapter-ligated library) onto a MinElute column and incubate for 00:02:00 . This allows the DNA sufficient time to bind to the silica membrane
Spin at 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flow-through as in step 3.4 .
Add 700 µL PE buffer to the MinElute column.
Spin at 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flow-through as in step 3.4 .
Dry spin at 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Flip columns by 180° and dry spin again at 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Remove columns from their collection tubes and place them in new 1.5 ml LoBind tubes.
Add 22 µL of pre-heated EBT to column, let stand for 00:01:00 , then spin 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute to elute. Carefully pipette EBT directly onto the center of the membrane without touching it.

Prepare adapter fill-in reaction calculating 40 µL, / reaction . Use a 1.5 ml LoBind tube to set up the adapter fill-in mastermix. A B C D 1 Reagent Stock concentration Final concentration 1x Volume [µl] 2 Isothermal Buffer 10 x 1 x 4 3 dNTPs 25 mM each 0.25 mM each 0.4 4 Bst Polymerase 8 U 0.4 U 2 5 UV HPLC-water 13.6 6 Eluate from step 7.10 20 7 Assay total 40 Do not add the eluate to the mastermix if you set it up for 2 or more reactions!
Add 20 µL of mastermix and the complete eluate (step 7.10) (~ 20 µL ) to each tube (use 0.2 ml PCR strips). Mix by flicking the tubes and spin down briefly.
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 then 80 °C for 00:10:00 in the thermocycler. During this incubation label new 0.2 ml tubes for step 10 and new 1.5 ml LoBind tubes for step 11.
Aliquot 18 µL water to the 0.2 ml PCR tubes. Aliquot 2 µL per library to 0.2 ml PCR tubes with 18 uL water (making a 1:10 dilution of the library) for the qPCR quality check (steps 13-14).
Transfer the remaining ~ 36 µL of final library to a fresh 1.5 ml LoBind tube.
Freeze the library at -20 °C without purification until further processing.

Prepare a qPCR assay calculating 20 µL, / reaction . Prepare 2 reactions per sample, plus 16 additional reactions for 7 qPCR standards in duplicates and 2 qPCR blanks. This qPCR uses a 1:10 dilution of the samples. The 1:10 dilutions of the samples are prepared in the cleanroom, but the qPCR is performed in the modern lab. %s %s **Important** Do NOT vortex the DyNAmo MasterMix, it will create bubbles that are nearly impossible to remove. The bubbles will interfere with the qPCR measurements. Gently pipette the DyNAmo MasterMix to equally distribute the fluorescent dye. Gently pipette the qPCR master mix, or gently invert the tube several times to mix. Again avoid creating any bubbles.

Add 19 µL mastermix and 1 µL 1:10 diluted libraries, standard, or water to a fresh 96-well qPCR plate. Seal with adhesive clear qPCR seal sheets and briefly spin down. Be sure to check that the reactions do not contain bubbles, because this will affect the qPCR readings and subsequent downstream calculations. If bubbles are present, briefly centrifuge the plate at maximum speed to remove them.
Amplify the qPCR reactions with the following program: %s %s The number of DNA copies determined with this qPCR is used to determine the amount of sample used as input for indexing protocols.

Prepare for mechanical shearing of extracted DNA. This protocol describes shearing to 150bp for Illumina sequencing using an ultrasonicator. If the library is to be sequenced with a 75bp kit, shear it to 150bp. If it will be sequenced with a 150bp kit, shear it to 300bp, or longer if desired.

Place up to 250 ng of extracted DNA in 50 µL ultrapure water into a microTUBE-50 AFA Fiber Screw-Cap tube. 250 ng is the recommended amount of DNA to be added for this protocol. More or less DNA can be used, but it will not necessarily be the desired length of 150bp. If more is used, the length will be longer, and if less is used, the length will be shorter. Any remaining sample that will not be sonicated should be stored at -20 °C . Make sure there are no air bubbles in the tube, as this will interfere with the sonication and inhibit shearing.

Place the Screw-Cap tube into the sonciator, and select the appropriate sonciation protocol based on the tube you are using and the desired length of the DNA fragments. To shear to 150bp with a Covaris M220 focused ultrasonicator using a 50 uL Screw Cap microTUBE, select the protocol DNA_0150_bp_050_ul_Screw_Cap_microTUBE in the sonication software. Run the program until it completes. Be sure to label the tubes on both the tube side and cap, as the sonication can partially rub off labels.

Remove the sonicated sample from the Screw Cap tube. Unscrew the cap, and use a 10uL pipette to suck up any droplets on the top of the cap. Then go around the rim of the tube and suck up any droplets on the rim. Then remove the liquid from the tube in 10uL intervals and place in a 1.5 ml LoBind tube. Be sure to move the fiber in the tube with the pipette tip to get all liquid. Discard the Screw Cap tube once all liquid is removed.
At this point, the sonicated DNA can be either immediately taken into the library preparation protocol (step 6), or stored at -20 °C until ready for use.
creator: James A Fellows Yates
